name: Suspicious New Instance of an Office COM Object
notes:
This rule detects an Office application creating an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc. This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
SIGMA Description: Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc. This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES '(winword|excel|powerpnt|msaccess|mspub|eqnedt32|visio)\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") == 'svchost.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious New Instance of an Office COM Object on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES '(winword|excel|powerpnt|msaccess|mspub|eqnedt32|visio)\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") == 'svchost.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious New Instance of an Office COM Object

Event description: Detects an Office application creating an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc. This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
